zh.lifeSummary: 當Rostislav "Pereyaslavl" Yurevich Rurik於1112年出生在俄羅斯時，他的父親Yuri I Dolgorukiy Vladimirovich Dolgorukis19歲，母親Anna Aëpovna of the Cumans21歲。他於1134年與Княгиня Новгородская, боярыня Якуневна在蘇聯烏克蘭吉也-吉也福斯卡亞結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1151年4月6日在俄羅斯Новгородская ОбластьВеликий Новгород諾福格洛德諾福格洛德享年39歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rostislav "Pereyaslavl" Yurevich Rurik于1112年出生在俄罗斯时，他的父亲Yuri I Dolgorukiy Vladimirovich Dolgorukis19岁，母亲Anna Aëpovna of the Cumans21岁。他于1134年与Княгиня Новгородская, боярыня Якуневна在苏联乌克兰吉也-吉也福斯卡亞结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1151年4月6日在俄罗斯Новгородская ОбластьВеликий Новгород諾福格洛德諾福格洛德享年39岁。

nameMeanings.name: Yurevich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Boris, Vladimir, Leonid, Mikhail, Lev, Dmitry, Igor, Anatoly, Arkadiy, Yefim, Arkady, Aleksandr.  Jewish Isaak, Naum, Yakov, Isak, Hyman, Sima, Zalman.
Jewish (from Belarus and Lithuania): variant of Horowitz , originating under Russian influence, since Russian has no h and alters it to g in borrowed words and names.

variants.name: Horowitz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Gershon, Hyman, Naftali, Mayer, Baruch, Meyer, Moshe, Shlomo, Yaakov, Avram.

Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from the town of Hořovice in central Bohemia, named with a short form of a Czech personal name such as Hořimir or Hořislav. This town was a major Jewish centre in the Middle Ages.
History: This is one of the old Ashkenazic surnames. In Eastern Europe, it was used by rabbinical families well before the mass adoption of surnames by local Jews at the turn of the 19th century.

nameMeanings.name: Rurik
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Slovak (mainly Jurík): from a pet form of the personal name Juraj, an equivalent of Latin Georgius (see George ).

variants.name: George
meanings: English, Welsh, French, and Romanian: from the personal name George, Latin Georgius, Greek Geōrgios, from an adjectival form, geōrgios ‘rustic’, of Greek geōrgos ‘farmer’. This became established as a personal name in classical times through its association with the fashion for pastoral poetry. Its popularity in western Europe increased at the time of the Crusades, which brought greater contact with the Orthodox Church, in which several Christian martyrs and saints of this name are venerated, in particular a saint believed to have been martyred at Nicomedia in AD 303, who, however, is at best a shadowy figure historically. Nevertheless, by the end of the Middle Ages Saint George had become associated with an unhistorical legend of dragon-slaying exploits, which caught the popular imagination throughout Europe, and he came to be considered the patron saint of England among other places. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. German Georg , Assyrian/Chaldean Giwargis, Gewargis , or Georgis , and Albanian Gjergji , and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Greek Georgiadis , Georgopoulos , Hatzigeorgiou ‘George the Pilgrim’, and Papageorgiou , Romanian Georgescu or Gheorghescu, Serbian Djordjevic . The name George is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Geevarghese and Varghese ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name George (see 1 above) as a surname.

variants.name: Jurick
meanings: Germanized or Americanized form of Slovak Jurík (see Jurik ).
Americanized form of Croatian Jurić, Slovenian and Slovak Jurič (see Juric ). Compare Yurick .

nameMeanings.name: Rostislav
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Rostislav "Pereyaslavl" Yurevich Rurik was born in 1112, in Russia, his father, Yuri I Dolgorukiy Vladimirovich Dolgorukis, was 19 and his mother, Anna Aëpovna of the Cumans, was 21. He married Княгиня Новгородская, боярыня Якуневна in 1134, in Kiev-Kievskaya, Ukraine, Soviet Union. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 6 April 1151, in Novgorod, Novgorod, velikiy novgorod, Novgorod Oblast, Russia, at the age of 39.

Il a épousé Княгиня Новгородская, боярыня Якуневна en 1134, à Kiev-Kievskaya, Ukraine, Union soviétique. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 6 avril 1151, à Novgorod, Novgorod, velikiy novgorod, Novgorodskaya Oblast', Russie, à l’âge de 39 ans.

Er hat 1134 in Kiev-Kievskaya, Ukraine, Sowjetunion Княгиня Новгородская, боярыня Якуневна geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 6. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rostislav "Pereyaslavl" Yurevich Rurik родился в 1112 г. в местности Россия, его отцу по имени Yuri I Dolgorukiy Vladimirovich Dolgorukis было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Anna Aëpovna of the Cumans, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Княгиня Новгородская, боярыня Якуневна в 1134 г. в местности киев-киевскея, Украина, Советский Союз. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 6 апреля 1151 г. в местности новгород, новгород, Великий Новгород, Новгородская Область, Россия в возрасте 39 лет.

Se casó con Княгиня Новгородская, боярыня Якуневна en 1134, en Kiev-Kievskaya, Ucrania, Unión Soviética. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 6 de abril de 1151, en Novgorod, Novgorod, velikiy novgorod, Novgorodskaya Oblast', Rusia, a la edad de 39 años.
